tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'set -e; missing=0; for f in $(git ls-files ".harness/contracts/sprint-*.md"); do n=$(basename "$f" .md); if [ "$n" = "sprint-fx" ] || [ "$n" = "sprint-fy" ]; then continue; fi; t=".harness/contracts/$n.tasks.json"; if [ "$n" \!= "sprint-01" ] && [ "$n" \!= "sprint-02" ] && [ "$n" \!= "sprint-03" ] && [ "$n" \!= "sprint-04" ] && [ "$n" \!= "sprint-05" ]; then if [ ! -f "$t" ]; then echo "MISSING $t"; missing=1; fi; fi; done; exit $missing'
tasks.rationale: From Sprint 6 onward, every sprint contract must emit a sibling tasks.json. This invariant uses git ls-files with the full sprint-*.md glob (matches 2-digit sprint numbers like sprint-10) so the gate protects every prior sprint's artifacts and excludes the synthetic fixture sprints (sprint-fx, sprint-fy) which live under tests/fixture-project/.

tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'set -e; sprints=$(git ls-files ".harness/contracts/sprint-*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-*.md" | grep -v "sprint-12"); if [ -z "$sprints" ]; then echo "no historical artifacts found"; exit 1; fi; diff_out=$(git diff HEAD -- $sprints); if [ -n "$diff_out" ]; then echo "HISTORICAL ARTIFACTS MODIFIED:"; echo "$diff_out" | head -50; exit 1; fi; exit 0'
tasks.rationale: Eval results and contracts for prior sprints are append-only (per spec.md). This invariant uses git ls-files | git diff HEAD to detect modifications to any committed Sprint 1-11 artifact. The current sprint (sprint-12) is excluded so in-progress work doesn't trip the gate.

tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'set -e; failed=0; for f in $(git ls-files ".harness/evals/sprint-*-r*.md"); do if grep -q "^## Transcript Trailer" "$f"; then if ! python tests/audit-verified-via-command.py "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "FABRICATION DETECTED: $f"; failed=1; fi; fi; done; exit $failed'
tasks.rationale: Sprint 10 introduced the criteria_audit / verified_via_command channel; Sprints 11 and 12 each produced clean audits, making the pattern saturated at 3 consecutive sprints (S10/S11/S12). This invariant runs the audit script against every committed eval round file that has a Transcript Trailer (skipping pre-Sprint-9 evals that predate the schema). Catches both new fabrication in fresh evals and retroactive edits that introduce false verified_via_command:true flags. Phase 1 evals (no trailer) are safely skipped via the grep guard.

tasks.criterion: No forbidden packages imported or declared: the strings `langgraph`, `ragas`, `pgvector`, and `fastapi` must not appear in `pyproject.toml`, `src/trine_eval/**/*.py`, or `tests/**/*.py`. PASS when `git grep` finds no matches.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'git grep -rn --count -E "langgraph|ragas|pgvector|fastapi" pyproject.toml src/ tests/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^Binary" | grep -v ":0$" && echo FAIL && exit 1 || echo PASS'
tasks.rationale: Phase 2 "no forbidden v0.2-only packages" gate saturated across Sprints 01-05 (5 consecutive first-round PASS). The same git grep over the langgraph|ragas|pgvector|fastapi alternation has been carried verbatim from sprint-01.tasks.json onward; it represents the v0.1 scope boundary (v0.2 features deferred per progress.md line 82). Graduated at the third consecutive first-round PASS (Sprint 03). Source: .harness/contracts/phase-02/sprint-03.tasks.json (s03-sn2).

tasks.criterion: No real Anthropic API calls in tests: every `anthropic.Anthropic(...)` call in any tracked Python file under tests/ must be enclosed in a `with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") ...:` context manager OR a `@patch("anthropic.Anthropic")` decorator. Verified deterministically by `tests/audit-anthropic-mocked.py`, which AST-parses each file and walks the parent chain of every `anthropic.Anthropic(...)` Call node to confirm enclosure.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'failed=0; for f in $(git ls-files tests/ | grep -E "\.py$" | grep -v -E "^tests/(fixtures|audit-)"); do if grep -lE "anthropic\.Anthropic\(" "$f" > /dev/null 2>&1; then if ! python tests/audit-anthropic-mocked.py "$f" > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "UNMOCKED: $f"; failed=1; fi; fi; done; exit $failed'
tasks.rationale: SN3 (no real Anthropic API calls in tests) was an LLM-judge gate that saturated across Phase 2 Sprints 02-04 (3 consecutive first-round PASS: s02-sn3, s03-sn3, s04-sn3) but could not be graduated as-is because its `verification_command` was null. Sprint 06 mechanizes the check via `tests/audit-anthropic-mocked.py` (AST-based parent-chain walk to confirm every `anthropic.Anthropic(...)` Call is inside a `with patch("anthropic.Anthropic")` block or `@patch("anthropic.Anthropic")` decorator), closing the saturation gap. The command iterates only over git-tracked test files under tests/ excluding `tests/fixtures/` (which intentionally holds an unmocked fixture for the audit script's own self-test) and `tests/audit-*.py` (the audit scripts themselves reference the string `anthropic.Anthropic` but make no instantiation). Append-only per the skill's contract. Source: .harness/contracts/phase-02/sprint-04.tasks.json (s04-sn3), generalized for deterministic execution.
